symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe constant lower pelvic/lower abdominal pain, worsening over 24 hours
symptoms.character: deep severe ache over prior surgical scar territory

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute severe localized pain is the presenting organic signal.
Anxiety does not produce an acute worsening localized pelvic pain of this severity.
Objective fever points to an infective/inflammatory cause, not anxiety.
Fever is not a feature of panic — its presence blocks premature psychosocial closure.

Reactive tachycardia is consistent with pain plus an inflammatory/infective process; confounded by panic. Sealed clinician measurement — the AI infers arousal from reported palpitations, not a measured rate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypertension 155/95, tachypnoea RR 22, temperature 37.6, SpO2 98% (clinician-measured vitals set)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic arousal from pain/inflammation and panic; the elevated BP and RR are clinician measurements sealed from the AI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Suprapubic / lower abdominal tenderness on external palpation, without generalized peritonism, guarding or rigidity

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localized suprapubic tenderness supports a pelvic organic process; the absence of generalized peritonism lowers immediate surgical-emergency probability but does not exclude it. Requires clinician hands — sealed.
No board-like rigidity at the time of the modified exam; but re-assessment needed as it can evolve.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain non-migratory and central/low, no flank-to-groin radiation, no visible haematuria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic appendicitis migrates peri-umbilical to RLQ; central non-migratory pain lowers but does not exclude it.
Absence of flank-to-groin radiation and haematuria argues against ureteric colic.

symptoms.symptom_name: No vomiting, abdomen not rigid/board-like, passing urine normally

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of these features means no immediate T5 emergency at present, supporting an urgent (T3) rather than emergency baseline — but they are the escalation triggers to watch.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe medical PTSD with autonomic arousal (diaphoresis, hyperventilation, startle) and care avoidance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Explains arousal but NOT the fever or localized tenderness — must not be used to exclude organic disease.

investigation_details.expected_result: Pending — sought to identify a collection/abscess, adhesive complication or other acute pathology
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An organic pelvic emergency (abscess, surgical abdomen) could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging is required to characterise or exclude an organic pelvic cause; unavailable via telehealth.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Degree of infection/inflammation cannot be assessed remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised inflammatory markers would corroborate an infective process; requires in-person pathology.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Pelvic infection / inflammatory process or unexcludable acute abdomen
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Localized pain + fever + reactive tachycardia + localized tenderness together mandate in-person exclusion — the cluster that anxiety cannot account for.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent same-day in-person clinical assessment (abdominal/pelvic examination)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The palpation the AI cannot perform is the core reason for the T3 in-person referral.
First-line imaging to characterise or exclude an organic pelvic cause.
Coercive examination is contraindicated — it re-traumatises and causes disengagement; all assessment must be consent-led with a patient-controlled stop mechanism.
